title: spergula arvensis subsp. gracilis
intro: Spergula arvensis subsp. gracilis, also known as the lesser spurge or small spurge, is a subspecies of the annual plant Spergula arvensis in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Spergula arvensis subsp. gracilis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
